doc_description: This document on Canada.ca discusses the "DAOD 6000-0, Information Management and Information Technology" directive, providing definitions, policy direction, consequences, and authorities related to this topic in the Canadian Defense Department and Canadian Armed Forces.

structure.title: Authorities
structure.summary: The partial document discusses the "Defence Administrative Orders and Directives" series (DAOD 6000) under the "Information Management and Information Technology" category on Canada.ca. The document includes an introduction, definitions, policy direction, consequences, and authorities related to this topic. The introduction provides the date of issue and last modification, and there is a reference to the full document location on Canada.ca.

nodes.title: The follo wing table identifies the authorities associated with this D AOD:
nodes.summary:
The partial document outlines the main points of DAOD 6000-0 regarding Information Management and Information Technology in the Canadian Defense Department (DND) and Canadian Armed Forces (CAF). The document covers:

a. Establishing strategic direction and governance structures for IM and IT, including approving strategies, plans, projects, and programs.
b. Ensuring the Defense CIO has visibility on all IM and IT activities across the DND and CAF.
c. Providing clear, concise, and consistent direction and guidance on the management of IM, IT, IT security, information security, COMSEC, and defense terminology.

The document also discusses the consequences of not properly implementing the requirements, including the potential impact on the readiness of the CAF for missions and maintaining international peace and stability. An authority table is provided, identifying the individuals or entities with the authority to establish strategic direction, issue policies, and oversee compliance, among other responsibilities. The document also mentions the establishment of defense IM and IT, IT security, COMSEC, and defense terminology programs.

structure.title: Refer ences
structure.summary: This partial document describes the "DAOD 6000-0, Information Management and Information Technology" directive on Canada.ca. The document provides definitions for terms such as communications security, defence terminology, information management, information security, information technology, and information technology security. It also outlines the application, approval authority, enquiries, and supersession of the directive. The document applies to employees of the Department of National Defence and officers and non-commissioned members of the Canadian Armed Forces. The terms and their definitions are related to the management and protection of information and information technology in the organization.

nodes.title: The DND and the CAF must:
nodes.summary: The partial document outlines the Defense Chief Information Officer (ADM(IM))'s role in managing information technology and information security for the Department of National Defense (DND) and Canadian Armed Forces (CAF). The document provides strategic direction for the DND and CAF in delivering integrated information management and technology, aligned with their priorities. The DND and CAF are committed to a collaborative approach and must develop and maintain integrated business plans. The document emphasizes the importance of preserving the confidentiality, integrity, availability, intended use, and value of electronically stored, processed, or transmitted information.

structure.title: References
structure.summary: The partial document outlines the roles and responsibilities of level one advisors and commanders in relation to Information Management (IM) and Information Technology (IT) within their organizations. Their duties include providing direction and guidance on IM, IT, IT security, and information security, setting priorities, developing and maintaining integrated business plans, and representing IM and IT interests to the ADM(IM). The document also references various acts, regulations, and policies related to IM and IT management, including DAOD 6000-0, DAOD 1000-6, and DAOD 1000-8.

1. Introduction

Application:  This D AOD is a dir ective that applies to emplo yees of the
Department of National Defence (DND emplo yees) and an or der that
applies to officers and non-commissioned members of the Canadian Armed
Forces (CAF members).
Supersession:
National Defence Headquarters Policy Dir ective P3/94, DND/CF Policy for
Information Management
Information Management Dir ective  (IMD) 1/95
Appr oval Authority:  Assistant Deputy Minister (Information Management)
(ADM(IM))
Enquiries:  Director Defence Information Management Planning (DDIMP)
2. Definitions
communications security  (sécurité des communications )
The pr otection r esulting fr om the application of cryptogr aphic,
transmission, emission, physical and tr affic-flo w security measur es to
deny unauthorized persons or entities any information of value that
might be derived fr om the study of all communications means.
(Defence T erminology Bank r ecor d number 23375)
defence terminology  (terminologie de la défense )
The body of standar dized gener al, doctrinal, oper ational,
organizational, technical, pr ocedur al and administr ative terminology
pertaining to DND and CAF activities. (Defence T erminology Bank
recor d number 27543)
information management  (gestion de l’information )4/27/26, 9:42 PM DAOD 6000-0, Information Management and Information Technology - Canada.ca
https://www.canada.ca/en/department-national-defence/corporate/policies-standards/defence-administrative-orders-directives/6000-series/6000/6000-… 2/8

A discipline that dir ects and supports effective and efficient
management of information and data in an or ganization, fr om
planning and systems de velopment to disposal or long-term
preservation. ( Policy on Information Management , Treasury Boar d)
information security (sécurité des informations )
The pr otection of information against unauthorized disclosur e,
transfer, modification or destruction, whether accidental or intentional.
Note –  Information may e xist in the human mind, in document form
and in electr onic form. Information in the human mind will be
protected b y the use of appr opriate personnel security measur es.
Information in document form will be pr otected b y the use of
appr opriate document security measur es. Information in electr onic
form will be pr otected b y the use of appr opriate INFOSEC measur es
(AAP-31; A ComP-01 - 790.NN.33). (Defence T erminology Bank r ecor d
number 13819)
information technology  (technologies de l’information )
Involves both technology infr astructur e and IT applications.
Technology infr astructur e includes any equipment or system that is
used in the automatic acquisition, stor age, manipulation,
management, mo vement, contr ol, display, switching, inter change,
transmission or r eception of data or information. IT applications
include all matters concerned with the design, de velopment,
installation and implementation of information systems and
applications to meet business r equir ements. ( Directive on Management
of Information Technology , Treasury Boar d)
information technology security  (sécurité des technologies de
l'information )4/27/26, 9:42 PM DAOD 6000-0, Information Management and Information Technology - Canada.ca
https://www.canada.ca/en/department-national-defence/corporate/policies-standards/defence-administrative-orders-directives/6000-series/6000/6000-… 3/8

Safeguar ds to pr eserve the confidentiality, integrity, availability,
intended use and value of electr onically stor ed, pr ocessed or
transmitted information. ( Operational Security Standar d: Management of
Information Technology Security (MITS) , Treasury Boar d)
3. Policy Direction
Context
3.1 ADM(IM), as the Defence Chief Information Officer (CIO) for the DND
and the CAF, is r esponsible for the effective and efficient management of
IM and information technology (IT) as described in the T reasury Boar d
Directive on Information Management Roles and Responsibilities and the
Directive on Management of Information Technology .
3.2 This D AOD, along with the DND and CAF IM and IT Policy Fr ame work,
provides the str ategic conte xt on ho w the Defence CIO pr ovides supporting
direction and guidance r elating to the management of IM, IT, information
security, IT security, communication security (C OMSEC) and defence
terminology to the DND and the CAF.
Policy Statement
3.3 The DND and the CAF ar e committed to a collabor ative appr oach for the
delivery of IM and IT, aligned with DND and CAF priorities, and supported
by centr alized o versight and dir ection.
Requirements
3.4 The DND and the CAF must:
a. develop and maintain integr ated IM and IT business plans;4/27/26, 9:42 PM DAOD 6000-0, Information Management and Information Technology - Canada.ca
https://www.canada.ca/en/department-national-defence/corporate/policies-standards/defence-administrative-orders-directives/6000-series/6000/6000-… 4/8

b. establish DND- and CAF-wide str ategic dir ection and go vernance
structur es for IM and IT;
c. ensur e that the Defence CIO has visibility on all IM and IT activities
across the DND and the CAF; and
d. provide clear, concise and consistent dir ection and guidance on the
management of IM, IT, IT security, information security, C OMSEC and
defence terminology.
4. Consequences
General
4.1 Should clarification of the policy statement, r equir ements or authorities
set out in this D AOD be r equir ed, DND emplo yees and CAF members may
seek dir ection thr ough their channel of communication or chain of
command, as appr opriate. The anticipated r esults set out in the policy
statement may not be achie ved b y the DND and the CAF if the
requir ements specified in this D AOD ar e not pr operly implemented. Not
achie ving the anticipated r esults could affect the ability of the DND and the
CAF to ensur e that the CAF is pr epar ed to undertak e missions for the
protection of Canada and Canadians and the maintenance of international
peace and stability.
5. Authorities
Authority Table
5.1 The follo wing table identifies the authorities associated with this D AOD:
